title: Sandwich ELISA
summary: Protocol for sandwich ELISA including sample preparation, capture antibody coating, blocking, sample addition, detection, and absorbance reading.
summary_en: Protocol for sandwich ELISA including sample preparation, capture antibody coating, blocking, sample addition, detection, and absorbance reading.

steps.text: Collect blood samples in tubes with anti-coagulant.
steps.text_en: Collect blood samples in tubes with anti-coagulant.
steps.verbatim: Collect blood samples in tubes with anti-coagulant.

steps.text: Centrifuge the samples at 1,000-10,000 x g for 10 min at 4°C and remove the pellet.
steps.text_en: Centrifuge the samples at 1,000-10,000 x g for 10 min at 4°C and remove the pellet.
steps.verbatim: Centrifuge the samples at 1,000-10,000 x g for 10 min at 4°C and remove the pellet.

steps.text: Aliquot the supernatant into several tubes (minimum 50 µL each).
steps.text_en: Aliquot the supernatant into several tubes (minimum 50 µL each).
steps.verbatim: Aliquot the supernatant into several tubes (minimum 50 µL each).

steps.text: Dilute the capture antibody in coating buffer to 1-10 µg/mL.
steps.text_en: Dilute the capture antibody in coating buffer to 1-10 µg/mL.

gaps.question: What is the exact final concentration to use within the 1-10 µg/mL range?

steps.verbatim: Dilute the capture antibody in coating buffer to 1-10 µg/mL.

steps.text: Add ~50 µL of diluted capture antibody to each well and cover the plate.
steps.text_en: Add ~50 µL of diluted capture antibody to each well and cover the plate.
steps.verbatim: Add ~50 µL of diluted capture antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature, or overnight at 4°C.

steps.text: Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature, or overnight at 4°C.
steps.text_en: Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature, or overnight at 4°C.
steps.verbatim: Add ~50 µL of diluted capture antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature, or overnight at 4°C.

steps.text: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.text_en: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.text_en: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Add 200 µL of blocking buffer to each well and cover the plate.
steps.text_en: Add 200 µL of blocking buffer to each well and cover the plate.
steps.verbatim: Add 200 µL of blocking buffer to each well and cover the plate. Block with gentle agitation for 1-2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Block with gentle agitation for 1-2 h at room temperature.
steps.text_en: Block with gentle agitation for 1-2 h at room temperature.

gaps.question: Should blocking be 1 h or 2 h?

steps.verbatim: Add 200 µL of blocking buffer to each well and cover the plate. Block with gentle agitation for 1-2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Dilute the samples, controls and standards in dilution buffer.
steps.text_en: Dilute the samples, controls and standards in dilution buffer.

gaps.question: What dilution factor should be used for samples, controls and standards?

steps.verbatim: Dilute the samples, controls and standards in dilution buffer.

steps.text: Add 100 µL of diluted samples and standards to the wells and cover the plate.
steps.text_en: Add 100 µL of diluted samples and standards to the wells and cover the plate.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of diluted samples and standards to the wells and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.text_en: Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of diluted samples and standards to the wells and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.text_en: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.text_en: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Add 100 µL of pre-diluted detection antibody to each well and cover the plate.
steps.text_en: Add 100 µL of pre-diluted detection antibody to each well and cover the plate.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of pre-diluted detection antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.text_en: Incubate for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of pre-diluted detection antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.text_en: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.text_en: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Add 100 µL of HRP-conjugated secondary antibody to each well.
steps.text_en: Add 100 µL of HRP-conjugated secondary antibody to each well.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of HRP-conjugated secondary antibody to each well and incubate for 1-2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate for 1-2 h at room temperature.
steps.text_en: Incubate for 1-2 h at room temperature.

gaps.question: Should secondary antibody incubation be 1 h or 2 h?

steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of HRP-conjugated secondary antibody to each well and incubate for 1-2 h at room temperature.

steps.text: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.text_en: Add wash buffer to each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.text_en: Discard the wash buffer from each well.
steps.verbatim: Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

steps.text: Add 50-100 µL of TMB enzyme substrate to each well.
steps.text_en: Add 50-100 µL of TMB enzyme substrate to each well.

gaps.question: Should 50 µL or 100 µL of TMB substrate be used?

steps.verbatim: Add 50-100 µL of TMB enzyme substrate to each well and incubate with gentle agitation on a plate shaker.

steps.text: Incubate with gentle agitation on a plate shaker.
steps.text_en: Incubate with gentle agitation on a plate shaker.
hazards: gentle agitation on a plate shaker
hazards_en: gentle agitation on a plate shaker

gaps.question: For how long should the TMB substrate be incubated?

steps.verbatim: Add 50-100 µL of TMB enzyme substrate to each well and incubate with gentle agitation on a plate shaker.

steps.text: Add 100 µL of stop solution to each well and shake for 1 min to mix.
steps.text_en: Add 100 µL of stop solution to each well and shake for 1 min to mix.
steps.verbatim: Add 100 µL of stop solution to each well and shake for 1 min to mix.

steps.text: Read the absorbance in the plate reader at 450 nm.
steps.text_en: Read the absorbance in the plate reader at 450 nm.
steps.verbatim: Read the absorbance in the plate reader at 450 nm.
